tests.intent: The four materialized relations and named SQL property graph expose deterministic keys, coordinates, ordering, and source attributes.
An independently decoded BLOCKMAP re-encodes byte-for-byte, preserving signed origins, shared offsets, list headers, and terminators.
Pinned origin, dimensions, cell count, world minima, and source word offsets agree with raw WAD bytes.
Every pinned block cell has the independently decoded ordered linedef list, including shared and empty lists.
Every materialized cell-to-linedef membership preserves cell id, line ordinal, and valid linedef identity.
Floor division at negative world coordinates selects correct cells immediately below, on, and above 128-unit boundaries.
All 182 source-sector bit rows match independent LSB-first row-major decoding of the exact 4,141-byte REJECT lump.
Known clear and set pairs include zero, final, spawn, and nontrivial sector indices in both directions.
A hand-authored four-sector graph includes passable two-sided lines, excludes closed and one-sided lines, and preserves sound blocking.
Each eligible physical connection creates exactly two inverse directed edges with the same linedef and attributes.
Pinned graph counts, openings, hashes, connected components, and isolated vertices match a raw-WAD oracle.
DOOM_SECTOR_GRAPH is a valid Oracle SQL property graph over sector vertices and passable directed edges.
Reachability is symmetric across paired directed edges, including a mini-map component and the pinned spawn component.
Linedef flag 0x40 is represented on both graph directions without changing topological passability.
Repeated materialization and reordered source reads yield identical ordered block, reject, and graph documents.
Production uses set-based SQL, raw source relations, exact bit arithmetic, SQL property graph DDL, and GRAPH_TABLE consumption.
Production cannot read evaluator artifacts, embed expected hashes or fixture coordinates, inspect callers, or forge success.
